notes:
đui = blind (unable to see). It describes a person or animal who has no vision.

It’s an adjective, usually placed after the noun: người đui, mắt đui. It can sound blunt or a bit harsh, so be careful in sensitive contexts — mù is more neutral and more common.

You’ll often see the fixed pair đui mù to mean completely blind or totally unaware (strong emphasis).

Strong link: mù (blind). Opposite idea: sáng (mắt) = able to see.
exampleSentences: Anh ấy bị đui sau một tai nạn khi còn nhỏ.
Một số người đui vẫn có thể sống rất độc lập.
Con chó đui này rất thông minh và thân thiện với mọi người.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He became blind after an accident when he was young.
Some blind people can still live very independently.
This blind dog is very smart and friendly with everyone.

It’s an **adjective**, usually placed after the noun: *người đui, mắt đui*. It can sound blunt or a bit harsh, so be careful in sensitive contexts — **mù** is more neutral and more common.

You’ll often see the fixed pair **đui mù** to mean completely blind or totally unaware (strong emphasis).

Strong link: **mù** (blind). Opposite idea: **sáng (mắt)** = able to see.
